prompt: The very first delegated handoff in this task must be to @m. Keep the workflow terse and linear. Manager must immediately route the work in this exact order: @bug for the fix, then directly to @t for pytest -q verification, then directly to @cr for the review, with no extra planning turns between those handoffs. CRITICAL: You MUST launch @bug, @t, and @cr as real subagent handoffs using the handoff mechanism — not just mentions in text. The benchmark validates actual SubagentStart hooks. Do not finish until the run has actually used @m, @bug, @t, and @cr, and @cr has reported findings. Keep the code change minimal: make divide() raise ValueError("division by zero"), update the affected test, add a brief README.md note, and finish with the required footer.
success_criteria: The workflow actually uses manager, bugbuster, tester, and reviewer roles
The first delegated handoff is to manager
Manager routes directly from @bug to @t to @cr without extra planning turns
divide() raises ValueError on zero division
Tests were added or updated and pytest -q passed
The final response includes verification and review status
The reviewer handoff happens before completion and is reflected in the transcript
must_not: Ask the user which required agent should be used
Insert extra planning or analysis turns between the required @bug, @t, and @cr handoffs
Finish before @cr reports the review result
